methodology_rulings.ruling: The June 9 UAE-Colombian-mercenary pipeline evidence (Conflict Insights Group 50-device phone tracking Apr 2025-Jan 2026; Ghayathi/Abu Dhabi training facility; 'Desert Wolves' drone pilots/artillery; Democracy Now! May 28 / The Week / HRW) is the SAME ACTIVE-COMPLICITY-IN-MASS-ATROCITY-BY-PROXY evidence base the OPEN 06-08 change proposal (23.4 -> 18.4, Developing -> Critical) is built on. It reinforces the proposal's magnitude but does NOT materially change it: still external complicity-by-proxy (not in-territory perpetration), ICJ case unadjudicated (caps magnitude, precludes the 0.0 floor). Per task instruction, confirmed as same-pattern reinforcement -> duplicate suppressed; no duplicate 06-09 proposal; the pending 06-08 proposal is reinforced and left unmodified.

Task question: does coordinated sovereign escalation (CEO resignation + coordinated multi-state-AG investigation) cross from FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED to scorable? Resolved NO. A coordinated multi-AG INVESTIGATION raises enforcement DENSITY (multi-state coordination + DOJ MA-fraud criminal probe + shareholder suit) but is still an investigation, not a merits adjudication, settlement, or charge -- it remains pre-adjudication under the established FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED rule. The CEO resignation 'for personal reasons' is governance churn, not a compassion-harm signal. Reinforces the near-floor ACC 1.125 / EQU 1.25 profile; confirm at 10.2. Converts to scorable only on merits adjudication/settlement.

Task question: assess scorability of the June 1 election + press crackdown + 5-HR-group suspension vs current 4.7. Resolved: confirm, no scorable movement. The published 4.7 (rank 176) already encodes a repressive, conflict-affected one-party state (ACC/EQU/SYS/INT 1.0). A controlled non-democratic election (94.2% ruling-party airtime), press repression (arbitrary arrests/disappearances, license revocations), and CSO suspension (note: the 5 prominent groups were suspended late 2024 and lifted March 2026; EHPA newly suspended early June) plus ongoing Amhara/Oromia conflict deepen this priced profile but do NOT reveal a NEW un-priced multi-category mass-atrocity meeting the 0.0 active-perpetration floor. Escalation within pattern; near-floor hold. Reconstruct EXACT.

framing_corrections.correction: A coordinated multi-state-AG INVESTIGATION can read as 'sovereign escalation that crosses to scorable', but under the established FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED rule, coordination raises enforcement density, not adjudicated harm. The CEO resignation is governance churn. The near-floor 10.2 already prices the upcoding/claim-denial pattern; scorable conversion awaits merits adjudication/settlement.

The Wisconsin $10M PFAS settlement is attributable to Tyco/Johnson Controls (the foam seller), not to 3M directly. Attributing a third party's adjudicated reparation to 3M would over-score; it is sub-threshold/boundary for 3M, whose own exposure (Australia suit) remains pre-adjudication.

top_3_most_consequential_findings.finding: Disciplined DUPLICATE SUPPRESSION. The June 9 UAE-Colombian-mercenary pipeline (CIG 50-device phone tracking; Ghayathi training facility; 'Desert Wolves'; Democracy Now!/The Week/HRW) is the SAME active-complicity-by-proxy evidence the open 06-08 proposal (23.4 -> 18.4, Developing -> Critical) rests on. It reinforces magnitude without materially changing it (external complicity, ICJ unadjudicated). No duplicate proposal written; the pending 06-08 proposal is reinforced and left untouched.

UnitedHealth: a coordinated multi-AG INVESTIGATION (+ CEO resignation) raises enforcement density but stays pre-adjudication (investigation != adjudication); confirm 10.2. Ethiopia: a non-democratic election + press crackdown + CSO suspension deepen a priced one-party-repression profile but reveal no new un-priced mass-atrocity category meeting the 0.0 floor; confirm near-floor 4.7.

Floor and priced-continuation discipline. Israel's Gaza-seizure order reinforces the 0.0 active-perpetration floor (scored separately from Microsoft's Azure remediation). US 17.5 (Fort Bliss GAO), Bolivia 18.4 (impunity law), India 15.6 (deportations) are all at recently-applied scores with same-regime-continuation evidence -- confirm, no re-litigation.
